# Job 5

## Verses (27)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Give now a cry for help; is there anyone who will give you an answer? and to which of the holy ones will you make your prayer? | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | For wrath is the cause of death to the foolish, and he who has no wisdom comes to his end through passion. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | I have seen the foolish taking root, but suddenly the curse came on his house. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | Now his children have no safe place, and they are crushed before the judges, for no one takes up their cause. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Their produce is taken by him who has no food, and their grain goes to the poor, and he who is in need of water gets it from their spring. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | For evil does not come out of the dust, or trouble out of the earth; | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | But trouble is man's fate from birth, as the flames go up from the fire. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | But as for me, I would make my prayer to God, and I would put my cause before him: | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | Who does great things outside our knowledge, wonders without number: | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Who gives rain on the earth, and sends water on the fields: | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | Lifting up those who are low, and putting the sad in a safe place; | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | Who makes the designs of the wise go wrong, so that they are unable to give effect to their purposes. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | He takes the wise in their secret designs, and the purposes of the twisted are cut off suddenly. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | In the daytime it becomes dark for them, and in the sunlight they go feeling about as if it was night. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | But he keeps safe from their sword those who have no father, and the poor from the power of the strong. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | So the poor man has hope, and the mouth of the evil-doer is stopped. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | Truly, that man is happy who has training from the hand of God: so do not let your heart be shut to the teaching of the Ruler of all. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | For after his punishment he gives comfort, and after wounding, his hands make you well. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | He will keep you safe from six troubles, and in seven no evil will come near you. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | When there is need of food he will keep you from death, and in war from the power of the sword. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | He will keep you safe from the evil tongue; and you will have no fear of wasting when it comes. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | You will make sport of destruction and need, and will have no fear of the beasts of the earth. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | For you will be in agreement with the stones of the earth, and the beasts of the field will be at peace with you. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | And you will be certain that your tent is at peace, and after looking over your property you will see that nothing is gone. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | You will be certain that your seed will be great, and your offspring like the plants of the earth. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | You will come to your last resting-place in full strength, as the grain is taken up to the crushing-floor in its time. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | See, we have made search with care, and it is so; it has come to our ears; see that you take note of it for yourself. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
